topic: LibSQL Local RAG Database and Symlink-Based Skill Evolution
context: We researched the second reference library, Cherry Studio, to design our local vector database and dynamic skill evolution mechanism.
options_considered: Use separate local databases (e.g. SQLite for chat history, LanceDB for vector store, and filesystem files for configuration)
Consolidate on LibSQL (SQLite fork with vector support) to handle relational data, vector search, and hybrid BM25 FTS, and implement symlink-based hot-reloading for local skills
chosen_option: Consolidate on LibSQL/SQLite for relational + vector + FTS database, and implement folder-based symlink hot-reloading for skills in /memory/skill_memory/.
reasoning: LibSQL is SQLite-compatible, meaning zero setup overhead for the user. Having relational schema, vector blobs, and FTS5 search in one single file database simplifies local state management.

Symlinking skill directories from /memory/skill_memory/ directly to the execution workspace allows the Agent to write and modify its own skills in-place, achieving instant hot-reload without building a complex plugin runtime.
tradeoffs: LibSQL vector bindings require native compilation, but package size is much smaller than spinning up heavy external vector DB processes.
future_risks: Ensuring stable precompiled binaries of LibSQL/SQLite-vector for all OS platforms during local setup.
